task:
Implement ISS-006: Incremental Updates for gid extract

**Context**: Currently `gid extract --lsp` rebuilds the entire code graph every time. Even changing one file triggers full re-parsing of all files + all LSP queries. This is especially painful with the LSP daemon where initial analysis takes ~8 minutes.

**Goal**: Detect file changes (mtime/content hash) and only re-extract + re-query LSP for modified files, merging results into the existing graph.

**Key design considerations**:
1. Store file metadata (path, mtime, content hash) alongside the graph so we can detect changes
2. On `gid extract`, compare current file state vs stored metadata to find added/modified/deleted files
3. Only run tree-sitter parsing + LSP queries on changed files
4. Merge new/updated nodes into existing graph, remove nodes from deleted files
5. Update stored metadata after successful extraction
6. Full rebuild should still be available as fallback (e.g. `--force` flag)

**Project location**: /Users/potato/clawd/projects/gid-rs/
**Main crate**: crates/gid-core/src/ (code_graph.rs, lsp_client.rs, lsp_daemon.rs are relevant)
**Existing tests**: tests/ directory

tasks: Implement LSP client module (lsp_client.rs) with stdio transport, initialize/shutdown lifecycle, and textDocument/definition queries
Modify code_graph.rs to integrate LSP for TypeScript call edge resolution with fallback to tree-sitter
Implement incremental extraction system with FileMetadata tracking, change detection, and graph merging
Add cache management (CachedCodeGraph serialization, cache location, invalidation logic)
Update CLI with --lsp flag and gid tools integration in ToolRegistry
Create test fixtures and metrics for LSP precision/recall and incremental update performance
